title: Phonics Flashcards
description: Learn each phonetic symbol with its example and articulatory description.

cards.example: Place your tongue between your teeth and blow air gently.
Like /θ/ but with vibration in the throat.
Open your mouth more than for "e" but less than for "a".
A relaxed and short "a". The mouth barely opens.
A relaxed "i", without tensing the lips.
The tongue does NOT touch anything. It curves back.
Just a breath of air, like fogging up a mirror.

title: Sound → Word
description: Match each phonetic symbol with the word that contains that sound.

title: Minimal Pairs Quiz
description: Can you distinguish between similar sounds? Test your hearing!

questions.question: The sound /θ/ is found in which word?
"Ship" has the vowel sound:
questions.explanation: Ship = /ʃɪp/ with short /ɪ/. Sheep = /ʃiːp/ with long /iː/.

questions.question: The vowel in "cat" is:
questions.explanation: Cat = /kæt/. The /æ/ sound is between "a" and "e".

questions.question: Which pair are minimal pairs?
questions.explanation: Ship /ʃɪp/ and sheep /ʃiːp/ differ only in one vowel sound.

questions.question: In English, "r" is pronounced by:
questions.explanation: English /ɹ/ — the tongue curls back but does NOT touch anything.

questions.question: Questions (yes/no) in English usually have:
questions.explanation: Yes/no questions rise at the end: "Do you like coffee?" ↗️

title: Intonation Words
description: Unscramble words related to questions and statements in English.

words.hint: Hot drink that many drink in the morning
Ask; in English the voice rises at the end (↗️)
Affirmation; the voice drops at the end (↘️)
